countries/ukraine.summary: KYIV STRIKES KILL AT LEAST 14 (WITH SEPARATE UN-DOCUMENTED INCIDENT KILLING 22, INCL. 4 CHILDREN) — SUSTAINED SURGE, HUMANITARIAN ACCESS NARROWING IN DONETSK (within window, continuing): Russian missile and drone attacks on Kyiv killed at least 14 and injured 80+, heavily damaging apartment buildings, days after a separate attack killed at least 30. UN Human Rights Monitors separately documented a strike killing at least 22 civilians and injuring 100+, including four children. Humanitarian access has been narrowing in Donetsk region amid intensifying fighting, and a humanitarian convoy was struck by drone in Dnipro region — the third such incident in two months, with attacks on humanitarian personnel/assets up 20%+ year-on-year.

countries/bolivia.summary: STATE OF EXCEPTION REMAINS IN EFFECT — ROADBLOCKS ENDED BUT UNDERLYING CONDITIONS AND JUSTICE-SYSTEM IMPUNITY UNCHANGED (within window, dated Jul 2 HRW report, sustained): HRW's Jul 2-dated report confirms the seven-week roadblock crisis (May-June, opposing agrarian reform and economic grievances) has ended, but President Paz's 90-day state of exception (declared Jun 20, allowing military deployment into the streets) remains in force. Long-standing impunity for at least 37 protest-related killings in 2019 continues, with HRW noting political interference in the investigation. No new dated escalation beyond this Jul 2 assessment was identified this cycle; the Bolivia critical-band calibration flag remains open (carried forward, not assessed).

countries/israel.summary: ISRAELI STRIKE ON GAZA FUNERAL (JUL 17) KILLS AT LEAST 7, WOUNDS 22 — CUMULATIVE POST-CEASEFIRE TOLL CONTINUES CLIMBING (within window, NEW dated incident): A local hospital reported an Israeli strike on a funeral in Gaza killed at least seven and wounded 22 on Jul 17, the most recent in a continuing pattern of ceasefire-period strikes (Jul 6: 6 killed; Jul 8: 8 killed incl. 2 children). The Gaza Government Media Office's cumulative post-ceasefire toll continues to rise from the 1,123 killed/3,616 injured figure logged 07-16. Floor sustained and reinforced by a fresh dated mass-casualty incident; cannot move lower.

countries/cuba.summary: GRID REMAINS COLLAPSED, BLACKOUTS 20-22 HRS/DAY, HRW COUNT (700+) AND PRISONERS DEFENDERS RECORD (1,200+/1,306 AS OF JUL 9) REAFFIRMED (within window, sustained/continuing): Cuba's national grid remains in its third-in-ten-days collapse (Jul 14, ~10M without power), its fifth nationwide blackout of 2026, driven by the US oil blockade compounding an overcentralized, aging system. Human Rights Watch's count of 700+ politically imprisoned and Prisoners Defenders' record of 1,200+ (1,306 as of Jul 9, already logged 07-16) were both reaffirmed by this cycle's search, with continued pot-banging protests across the island. No new record count beyond the Jul 9 figure already priced.

countries/nigeria.summary: NO NEW DATED ESCALATION BEYOND THE ALREADY-LOGGED JULY WFP CUT — 36.2M FOOD INSECURE, 300,000+ CHILDREN AFFECTED BY NUTRITION-PROGRAM SCALE-DOWN (within window, sustained, no fresh own-conduct trigger): Search results reaffirmed the same figures logged 07-16: WFP forced to scale down northeast nutrition programs in July (300,000+ children affected), 36.2M nationally food insecure, 17M+ across nine northern states at crisis/emergency/catastrophe levels, Borno's ~10,000 in catastrophic Phase-5 conditions. WFP can support only 740,000 of 6.2M in acute need across the three worst-affected northeast states; Boko Haram's farming ban in Borno continues as a non-state-actor threat vector. Donor-funding-driven, substantially external; not a discrete new Nigerian own-conduct failure.

countries/uganda.summary: MIXED SIGNAL: EBOLA RECOVERY MILESTONE (LAST PATIENT DISCHARGED JUL 16, 42-DAY COUNTDOWN BEGUN) OFFSET BY WHO CRITICISM OF MARBURG TRANSPARENCY (within window, mixed): Uganda discharged its last Ebola patient from the Mulago National Referral Isolation Centre on Jul 16 (20 confirmed cases, 2 confirmed + 1 probable death; no new case since Jun 21) and began the 42-day countdown to declaring the outbreak over — a genuine positive development.
